purpose: SharePoint information architecture for the Global HR metadata-first platform, mapping HR domains, document libraries, managed metadata, content types, security groups, Power BI dashboards, Dataverse tables, APIs, and governance controls.

summary.security_pattern: Hub-and-domain SharePoint architecture with managed metadata, least-privilege security groups, retention labels, and explicit links to Dataverse/API/Power BI assets.

architecture_principles: Use SharePoint as the governed portal and evidence layer, not as the system of record for transactional HR data.
Use the Global HR Intelligence Hub for navigation, standards, catalogs, and cross-domain documentation.
Use domain-associated sites for controlled HR domain documentation and dashboard artifacts.
Apply metadata consistently across documents so SharePoint views can behave like an intelligent catalog.
Do not store secrets, API keys, client secrets, tokens, or raw sensitive exports in document libraries.
External demo access must use anonymized and aggregated content only.

hub_site.purpose: Central SharePoint hub for Global HR data product documentation, dashboard artifacts, metadata governance, Dataverse implementation assets, and API/security documentation.
hub_site.external_sharing: Disabled by default; demo-only external sharing through dedicated anonymized demo site.
hub_site.associated_sites_policy: All associated sites inherit hub navigation, common metadata taxonomy, branding, and governance links.

site_columns.name: GHR Domain
site_columns.description: HR domain or data product area.

site_columns.name: GHR Data Product ID
site_columns.description: Data product contract identifier such as GHR-DP-003.

site_columns.name: GHR Source Table
site_columns.description: Source CSV/table represented by the artifact.

document_libraries.name: 01 Data Product Contracts
document_libraries.purpose: Stores governed contracts, SLAs, ownership and consumption rules.

document_libraries.name: 02 Dashboards and Reports
document_libraries.purpose: Stores exported reports, dashboard briefs, screenshots and executive packs.

document_libraries.name: 03 Dataverse Build Assets
document_libraries.purpose: Stores Dataverse build plans, table designs, lookup definitions and deployment notes.

document_libraries.name: 04 API Specifications
document_libraries.purpose: Stores REST/OData specs, endpoint examples, payload mappings and integration notes.

document_libraries.name: 05 Governance and Quality
document_libraries.purpose: Stores DQ reports, lineage, validation results, issue logs and stewardship evidence.

document_libraries.name: 06 Policies and SOPs
document_libraries.purpose: Stores access policies, operating procedures, review records and compliance material.

permission_model.inheritance: Hub navigation is inherited; permissions are managed at domain site and sensitive library levels.

default_permission_levels.Members: Edit or Contribute based on mapped CRUD

labels.label: HR Internal - 3 Years
labels.applies_to: Internal HR documentation and reference artifacts

labels.label: HR Confidential - 5 Years
labels.applies_to: Confidential HR domain documents, dashboards, and SOPs

labels.label: HR Restricted - 7 Years
labels.applies_to: Compensation, Payroll, Audit, Succession, and restricted governance evidence

dlp_notes: Block upload of credential files and secret values.
Prevent external sharing of restricted/confidential documents.
Require sensitivity labels for exported reports and governance evidence.

recommended_navigation.label: Security & Access
recommended_navigation.target: Security Model page / 06 Policies and SOPs

actions: Create common branding and landing page
Map owners and members from security model

validation_checklist: All domain sites are associated with the hub.
All libraries use required GHR metadata columns.
All restricted domains have external sharing disabled.
All dashboard artifacts have Data Product ID and Classification metadata.
Dataverse table and API resource metadata exists for each mapped source table.
No secrets, API keys, client secrets, tokens, or raw credential files are stored in SharePoint.
Retention labels are applied based on classification.
